summary: [deterministic] all deterministic checks passed for engineering-vllm-inference-blueprint | [judge] [fallback from codex] All nine success criteria are met. cargo check/clippy/fmt pass clean, 32 tests pass across config (6), health (6), billing (11), and server (8) modules. Config-driven vLLM endpoint via OnceLock, tokio BufReader stderr draining, semaphore concurrency limiting, CI workflow, and billing limit enforcement are all correctly implemented. Two design observations remain but are not blocking defects.

findings.title: Off-chain signature verification does not validate recovered signer against on-chain spending key
findings.body: verify_spend_auth_signature returns Some(recovered_address) but the caller in server.rs only checks is_none() — it never verifies the recovered address is the authorized spending key for the commitment (via on-chain getAccount). Any valid EIP-712 signature from any private key will pass the off-chain check as long as operator matches and it hasn't expired. Inference is served before the on-chain authorizeSpend call, so an attacker can get free inference by signing with any key — the on-chain claim will fail afterward, but work was already done.
findings.evidence: server.rs:204-221 — recovered address is discarded. billing.rs:168 docstring says 'the caller can verify the signer is the legitimate spending key (via on-chain getAccount)' but this is never done.

findings.title: On-chain job handler hardcodes model name 'default' instead of reading from config
findings.body: The run_inference on-chain job handler at lib.rs:100 sends model: 'default' to vLLM. The HTTP server path correctly uses req.model.as_deref().unwrap_or(&self.config.vllm.model). The on-chain handler should use the configured model name from VLLM config, but it has no access to config (only VLLM_BASE_URL is stored in OnceLock). In practice vLLM instances serve a single model so this works, but it's inconsistent.
